commit.message: Add detailed Discord description to Community section

Add release announcements link, consolidate Community section

Collapse duplicate Support section into Community. Add link to
release announcements signup at primeradiant.com/superpowers/.

Rewrite README for current architecture

Replace loop-by-loop development docs with current usage, install,
and architecture documentation reflecting streaming ASR, Qwen 3.5
cleanup, uinput injection, and the GNOME Shell extension.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

GNOME Shell status pill overlay, fix uinput helper auto-spawn, batch size fix

- Status pill rendered via St widgets inside the GNOME Shell extension
  (addTopChrome with reactive:false — non-focusable, click-through,
  always above windows). Shows Recording.../Transcribing.../Cleaning up...
  and hides immediately when idle.
- Fix uinput helper stale socket: remove socket on any connection failure
  (including ConnectionRefused from dead process) before respawning.
  Also clean up stale socket at app startup.
- Fix cleanup helper GGML_ASSERT crash: increase n_batch from 512 to 2048
  to handle large prompts with corrections and OCR context.
- Disable screenshot+OCR fallback (triggers GNOME screenshot sound/notification).
  AT-SPI text access is the primary context source.
- StatusPill in the Rust app is a no-op on GNOME (layer shell not supported
  by Mutter). All overlay rendering delegated to the GNOME Shell extension.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix screenshot XML validation for GNOME 50, simplify cleanup prompt

- Screenshot contract validator now checks for argument names independently
  of XML attribute order (GNOME 50 emits type before name, breaking the
  exact-string match). OCR context capture should now work.
- Simplified cleanup prompt for 0.8B model: numbered rules instead of
  examples, explicit instruction to keep all sentences.
- Cleanup helper pre-warms at app startup (model load + context creation +
  system prompt decode happens before first recording).
- Context reused across generate calls, saved back to warm_ctx after each
  inference to avoid expensive re-creation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update smoke tests for parakeet-rs backend name

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pepper X Ghost Pepper parity: streaming ASR, Qwen 3.5 cleanup, full settings overhaul

Major changes:
- Migrate ASR from sherpa-onnx to parakeet-rs with Nemotron 0.6B streaming model
  (transcription during recording, 0ms post-recording latency)
- Migrate cleanup LLM from llama_cpp 0.3.2 to llama-cpp-4 0.2.23 with Qwen 3.5
  models, running in a subprocess to avoid symbol collision with ONNX Runtime
- Cleanup helper is a persistent daemon with KV cache prefill at app startup
  (0.5s cleanup vs 10s before)
- Evdev-based modifier capture replacing broken AT-SPI keyboard monitoring
- Configurable trigger keys (Alt+Super, Ctrl, Right Alt, or custom recorded combo)
- Hold-to-record and toggle-to-record modes with dual independent shortcut recorders
- Text insertion via XKB-aware uinput keystroke injection
- Settings restructured into 7-section sidebar (Recording, Cleanup, Corrections,
  Models, History, General, Diagnostics)
- Transcription lab with per-stage model pickers, inline prompt editor, word-level
  diff highlighting, audio playback, diarization timeline
- Sound effects on recording start/stop via canberra-gtk-play
- OCR context prefetch at recording start
- Floating overlay with colored status indicators
- GNOME extension icon states for recording/working/error/ready
- 200ms recording flush delay, performance trace logging
- Speaker diarization (energy-based V1)
- Post-paste correction learning, editable corrections with preferred transcriptions
  and commonly misheard replacements
- Model download with progress bar, auto-discovery of helper binaries

Performance: record=3.2s transcribe=0.0s cleanup=0.5s insert=0.2s total=0.7s
(down from record+transcribe=5s cleanup=10s total=15s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: type-safe ErrorSource union, stable React keys in error panel

- Add ErrorSource type ("run" | "fanout") instead of bare string

feat: server error log with UI panel

- In-memory ring buffer (ErrorLog, capacity 50) collects errors from
  run and fanout routes
- GET /api/errors endpoint returns errors newest-first
- Error indicator in header bar with red badge count
- Click to open dropdown panel showing source, timestamp, and message
- Auto-refreshes every 10 seconds; also refreshes on click

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: persistent "Running" banner in sidebar for live run navigation

When a test run is in progress, a pulsing teal banner appears at the
top of the sidebar showing the card title. Clicking it navigates back
to the live run view. Hidden when there's no active run or on error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: unify button terminology to "Generate Test Cards from ..."

Consistent language: both buttons now say "Generate Test Cards from
Observations" and "Generate Test Cards from Failure" instead of mixed
"Generate from Observations" / "Analyze Failure".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace vanishing toast with persistent inline fanout success banner

The "Generated N cards" toast disappeared after 2 seconds. Now shows a
persistent green banner listing generated card titles as links to the
cards view. Dismissible manually.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: show error in LiveRun when run fails to start

Previously api.run.start() errors were silently swallowed, leaving the
user on a "Connecting..." screen with no escape. Now errors are caught
and displayed inline with a "Back to Runs" button.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: config API endpoint with model dropdown in UI

- GET /api/config returns available models from GAUNTLET_MODELS (comma-
  separated) or GAUNTLET_AGENT_MODEL env vars
- NewRunModal shows dropdown when models are configured, text input

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: card parsing strips code fences, splits on frontmatter, logs failures

LLMs sometimes wrap generated story cards in markdown code fences or
omit ---CARD--- separators. splitAndValidateCards now:
- Strips ```yaml/``` fences before parsing
- Falls back to splitting on YAML frontmatter boundaries (---\nid:)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Docker arm64, Bun timeout, tool result text field bug

- Switch Dockerfile from google-chrome-stable (amd64-only) to chromium
  (multi-arch) for arm64 compatibility
- Add idleTimeout: 255s to Bun.serve() so LLM calls don't get killed
- Fix tool_result content blocks missing required text field when
  chrome.evaluate() returns undefined (JSON.stringify(undefined) → undefined)
- Add defensive ?? "" fallback in both Anthropic and OpenAI tool result
  formatters
- Extract anthropicToolResultMessages as exported function for testability

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix hover preview stuck, shelf docking, and grid snapping

Preview: hidePreview() called when container is removed or destroyed.
No more stuck previews when a stamp is restored.

Shelf: placeWindowAt() now respects reserved areas (blocks full-size
windows from shelf zone). Stamps pass allowReserved=true so they can
use the shelf cells. Full-size windows can't be dragged into the shelf.

Snapping: _finishFullSizeDrag falls back to _snapWindowToGrid if the
polling hadn't determined a target cell yet. Ensures windows always
snap to grid after any move.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix shelf placement, z-ordering, and hover preview visibility

Shelf: Grid now supports reserved areas — shelf mode reserves edge
columns/rows so full-size windows don't occupy them. Stamps are placed
in the reserved area. Full-size window placeWindow() skips reserved
cells. Stamp placeWindow() passes allowReserved=true.

Z-ordering: Accepted that window_group stacking is unreliable for
non-MetaWindowActors. Stamps go in top_window_group. Since the grid
engine guarantees stamps and full-size windows occupy separate cells,
there's no spatial overlap — z-order is irrelevant.

Hover preview: Only fires on stamp containers, which are in their own
non-overlapping cells. No phantom previews through windows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve placement settings UI with subtitles and conditional visibility

- Added descriptive subtitles to Stamp Placement, Shelf Edge, and
  Max Shelf Depth rows so users understand what each option does
- Shelf Edge and Max Shelf Depth rows now only show when Shelf
  placement mode is selected (hidden for In Place and Pack modes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stamps below windows, hover preview on stamps

Z-ordering: Stamp layer moved to bottom of window_group (not
top_window_group). Mutter only restacks its own MetaWindowActors,
so our St.Widget stays at the bottom naturally. No restacked handler
needed. Stamps now render below full-size windows.

Hover preview: Hovering over a stamp shows a full-size live clone
positioned above the stamp (clamped to monitor bounds). The preview
appears in top_window_group so it renders above everything. Hidden
on mouse leave, during drag, and during Overview.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use window top-left for grid cell calculation, not cursor position

The drag overlay was highlighting the cell under the mouse cursor,
but the window's top-left corner determines which grid cell it
occupies. Now uses metaWindow.get_frame_rect() during drag polling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Grid cells shown individually, windows snap to grid on any move

Overlay: Each cell the window will cover is shown as a separate
outlined rectangle with rounded corners and margin, instead of one
big highlight rectangle.

Snapping: Windows now snap to the nearest grid cell on ANY grab-op-end
(move or resize), not just when using our drag overlay. This means
dragging a window normally also snaps it to the grid. The window is
resized to exactly fill its grid cells.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stamp rendering: use top_window_group with stable z-ordering

Putting stamps at the bottom of window_group caused them to vanish
under Mutter's restacking. Instead, use top_window_group with
insert_child_below for stable positioning. Stamps occupy their own
grid cells so they don't overlap full-size windows spatially —
z-ordering only matters during transitions.

No more restacked signal handler, no more reentrancy guard, no more
glitching.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Put stamps below full-size windows, not above

Stamps are background items (like desktop icons), not overlays.
Move stamp layer to the bottom of window_group so full-size windows
render on top. Re-lower on Mutter restack events. During drag, the
dragged stamp is temporarily raised above everything.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stamp z-ordering and workspace awareness

Z-ordering: Move stamp layer from global.window_group (fights Mutter
restacking) to global.top_window_group (inherently above all windows).
Removes the restacked signal handler and reentrancy guard entirely.

Workspace: Add active-workspace-changed handler that hides stamp
containers for windows not on the active workspace. Stamps now
correctly appear/disappear when switching workspaces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix drag overlay and stamp release issues

- Grid overlay uses workarea (excludes panel) instead of monitor geometry
- Overlay highlights the full window span (e.g. 3x2 cells) not just
  the single cell under the cursor
- Fix stamp drag release: extension.js release handler now returns
  EVENT_PROPAGATE during drag so the dragHandler's own release handler
  (connected via Clutter.grab) can process the drop
- All window positioning uses workarea coordinates consistently

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix GNOME 50: get_maximized() -> get_maximize_flags()

MetaWindow.get_maximized() was renamed to get_maximize_flags() in
GNOME 50/Mutter 18. This caused the zoom keybind handler to crash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Change default keybinds to Super+=/Super+- (no numpad needed)

- Default zoom-in changed from <Super>KP_Add to <Super>equal
- Default zoom-out changed from <Super>KP_Subtract to <Super>minus
- Prefs keybind entries show apply button for clearer UX

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix prefs.js relocatable schema lookup

Same fix as extension.js — use Gio.SettingsSchemaSource.new_from_directory
to find the monitor schema in the extension's schemas/ dir. The prefs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Covers: unit tests, installation, live testing checklist (10 scenarios),

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix auto-shrink not triggering, remove debug tracing

- Call _evaluateAutoShrink() after each _onWindowReady() so windows
  beyond the focus-rank threshold get auto-shrunk as they appear
- Add first-frame fallback timeout (500ms) for headless/no-render

- Remove all debug tracing (/tmp/mb_trace.txt, _debugWriteState)

Verified: 28 unit tests pass, extension enables/disables cleanly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix GNOME 50 API incompatibilities found in integration testing

- Replace global.display.get_monitor_workarea() (doesn't exist) with

- Replace global.display.get_monitor_connector() (doesn't exist) with

- Fix relocatable GSettings schema lookup to use extension's schema

- Fix grab-op-begin signal handler to handle both 2-param and 3-param

Verified: extension enables (state 1) and disables (state 2) cleanly
in headless gnome-shell --virtual-monitor 1920x1080 with no errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

12 tasks covering all 7 modules:

4. Stamp placer (3 strategies + tests)
5. Focus tracker (rank list + tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address round 5 review: stamp drag cleanup and grid reconfig lossiness

- Stamp drag uses Clutter.grab() for reliable event capture instead of
  raw global.stage signal connections
- Grab handle stored and dismissed on release or unmanaging cleanup
- _dragFinalizing flag suppresses size-changed handler during drag
  finalization to prevent racing with move_resize_frame
- unmanaging cleanup explicitly calls grab.dismiss() if drag is active
- Grid reconfiguration span clamped to grid dimensions
- Acknowledged lossy nature of grid remapping as a limitation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address round 4 review: fix clone scaling, drag, signals, lifecycle

Key fixes:
- Clone scaling uses allocation (not scale_x/scale_y) per Clutter docs:
  "draws with paint function of another actor, scaled to fit its allocation"
- Stamp drag uses manual Clutter events (not grab-op, which only fires
  for MetaWindows, not St.Widgets)
- GrabOp detection uses equality check not bitwise AND (base bit shared
  with resize ops)
- Cross-monitor non-drag detection via window-entered-monitor signal
- Monitor hotplug via Main.layoutManager monitors-changed signal
- Window type filtering: only META_WINDOW_NORMAL and META_WINDOW_DIALOG
- Window actor availability: window-actor-added + first-frame deferral
- activate() requires timestamp (global.get_current_time())
- Keybindings removed in disable() via removeKeybinding()
- Restacked handler has reentrancy guard
- Focus events during Overview are queued, not processed
- Stamp layer hidden during Overview to avoid animation interference
- Single authoritative restore path via focusTracker (not click handler)
- monitor-connectors key added to schema table
- Fullscreen no longer "suspends" grid (vague); windows retain state
- Grid cell size from monitor workarea
- Unminimize detected via notify::minimized
- Focus events for unplaced windows ignored
- Zoom to 1.0 creating auto-eligible window documented as intended

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address round 3 review: fix rendering, input, lifecycle, and edge cases

Key fixes:
- Real actor uses opacity=0 (not visible=false) so clone renders while
  actor is invisible. Fixes ghost image when stamp repositioned.
- Clone uses scale_x/scale_y on clone actor (Clutter.Clone does NOT
  auto-scale to fit allocation — it paints at source size by default)
- Track source geometry changes via size-changed signal
- Prefs.js uses Gdk.Display for monitor enumeration (separate GTK process
  cannot access global.display)
- Window creation deferred to first-frame (window-created fires before
  actor has stable geometry)
- GNOME 50 is Wayland-only: remove X11 tool references
- Add HiDPI: all grid calculations use logical pixels
- Add minimized window handling
- Add null-focus handling
- Add window resize (user and application) handling
- Add non-drag cross-monitor move handling
- Document exempt as intentionally per-app
- Defer move_resize_frame with idle_add to avoid fighting Mutter
- Overview uses showing/hidden with reentrancy guard
- Drag uses pointer polling against monitor geometry (not window-entered-monitor)
- Keybind GSettings type is as (array of string) for addKeybinding()
- Batch shrink capped at 5 windows, rank-ordered selection
- Track previousCellAllocation for position restore
- Handle clone destruction during active drag
- Minimum grid size 4x3 enforced
- Acknowledge single-frame z-order flicker
- Add monitor-connectors GSettings key for prefs↔extension bridge

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address round 2 review: fix real issues, debunk false ones, target GNOME 50

Verified: Shell.util_set_hidden_from_pick and grab-op-begin/end DO exist
and are stable. Three reviewers were wrong about these being missing/removed.

Real fixes:
- Clone scaling uses allocation, not scale_x/scale_y (Clutter.Clone fills its allocation)
- Real actor stays visible (visible=false breaks clone rendering)
- Clones only for scaled windows, not full-size (resolves contradiction)
- App ID via Shell.WindowTracker (works for GTK, Qt, Electron, Flatpak)
- Hysteresis only blocks indirect restores, not direct user clicks
- Z-ordering via dedicated stamp layer in global.window_group
- Drag overlay as window_group child (not addChrome, which steals input)
- Position tracking via pointer polling (position-changed unreliable during grabs)
- Monitor connector via global.display.get_monitor_connector()
- dconf paths use slashes, connector names sanitized
- Grid reconfig handles multi-cell windows (recalc span, rank-order placement)
- Batched shrinks for rapid window creation
- Maximized windows implicitly exempt from auto-shrink
- Tiled windows: tile state preserved through stamp/restore cycle
- Overview suspend on 'showing' signal (before animation captures actors)
- Clone source destruction handled via MetaWindow 'unmanaging' signal
- Full extension enable/disable lifecycle specified
- Window-close restoration ignores hysteresis (not a focus event)
- Monitor reconnection state cached for 30 minutes
- Hardware overlay limitation documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Plan 1: walking skeleton implementation

First of 7 planned plans. Builds the thinnest possible end-to-end
pipeline of 6 skills with trivial stubs + artifact validators + sample
spec + dogfood procedure. Every sophisticated behavior (PAR, map-reduce
extraction, two-tier audit, scope review, interrupt handling) is
deferred to Plans 2-7 with explicit callouts.

15 tasks, all TDD-structured (failing test → implementation → passing
test → commit). Applies the plugin's own walking-skeleton philosophy
to bootstrapping the plugin itself.

Initial commit: iterative-development plugin design

Design doc for a new Claude Code plugin that pairs with superpowers,
providing an alternative implementation methodology for large or
comprehensive specs where the current writing-plans → SDD flow loses
the plot.

Core ideas:
- Extract requirements from arbitrary-size spec collateral (map-reduce
  over parallel subagents, up to 50MB prose)
- Story cards + epics as the unit of requirement (agile backlog model)
- Walking skeleton first, then iterate by sprint
- Parallel adversarial review at every evaluative gate
- Audit after every sprint (deep new work + light whole-product sweep)
- Guarded autonomous Ralph loop, resumable via re-invocation
- All process state in three artifacts under docs/superpowers/iterations/

Fix ghost cells blocking window placement

removeWindow() now clears ALL cells with the window's ID, not just
the cells within the recorded allocation. This handles cases where
the window's span changed (e.g., full-size to stamp) but old cells
from the previous larger span weren't cleared, leaving "ghost" cells
that blocked placement at those positions.

Also added findGhostCells()/clearGhostCells() diagnostic methods.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Swap windows when dropping on occupied cells, remove debug logging

When dragging a full-size window onto cells occupied by another
window, the occupant is evicted to the dragged window's old position
(swap). If the old position doesn't fit, falls back to best-fit.
This fixes windows being unable to reach certain grid positions
(like the top-left corner) when another window was already there.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix snapping for all shelf edges (left, right, top, bottom)

Replaced effectiveCols/effectiveRows with getUsableArea() which
returns {minCol, minRow, maxCol, maxRow} accounting for where the
shelf is. A left shelf shifts minCol, a top shelf shifts minRow,
etc. Fixes windows unable to snap to the edge opposite the shelf.

Applied consistently in drag handler (_updateDragPosition,
_snapWindowToGrid) and _onDropFullSize.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix drag-to-shelf and left-edge snapping

Drag-to-shelf: Track cursor position (not window top-left) to detect
when the user drags toward the shelf. Window top-left can never reach
the shelf because of span-based clamping, but the cursor can. When
cursor is in the reserved shelf area on drop, the window is minimized.

Left-edge snapping: Window position clamping now uses effectiveCols/
effectiveRows (which exclude the reserved shelf area) instead of raw
grid dimensions. A 3-column window on an 8-column grid with 2-column
right shelf now clamps to column 0-3 instead of 0-5, preventing
placeWindowAt from rejecting the placement due to shelf overlap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dragging a window into the shelf dock auto-minimizes it

When a full-size window is dropped on the reserved shelf area,
it is minimized (which triggers _onMinimizedChanged and creates
a stamp on the shelf). Works for both tracked drag drops and
snap-to-grid fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add option to disable auto-shrink by focus rank

New GSettings boolean 'auto-shrink-enabled' (default: true). When off,
windows are only stamped when manually minimized — no automatic
shrinking based on focus rank. The focus rank threshold spinner hides
when auto-shrink is disabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix windows not snapping to left/top edge

pixelsToCell() returned negative column/row when the window's
top-left was above or left of the workarea origin. Added Math.max(0)
clamp so negative pixel positions map to column/row 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Terminal unminimize-all bug, add stamp-to-window swap

Bug fix: Clicking a minimized stamp now calls unminimize() before
activate() to avoid GNOME's GtkApplication group behavior where
activating one window unminimizes all windows of the same app.

New feature: Dragging a stamp onto a full-size window swaps them.
The stamp is restored to the full-size window's position and the
full-size window is minimized (becoming a stamp on the shelf).
Uses grid.windowAtCell() to detect occupied target cells.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix addChrome params for GNOME 50

GNOME 50's addChrome() doesn't accept affectsInputRegion or
affectsStruts params (throws 'Unrecognized parameter'). Use
addChrome() with no options — defaults are fine for our use case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move stamp layer to chrome to fix meta_window crash

GNOME Shell's layout.js iterates window_group children and accesses
actor.meta_window. Our St.Widget stamp layer has no meta_window,
causing TypeError crashes in _windowsRestacked and _updateRegions.

Fix: Use Main.layoutManager.addChrome() instead of adding to
window_group or top_window_group. Chrome actors live in the UI layer
and are not iterated by GNOME Shell's restacking code. Previews also
use addChrome/removeChrome.

This eliminates the 'actor.meta_window is undefined' error spam and
should fix the Terminal showing up live (the crashes were preventing
clone creation from completing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix preview stuck on restore with 300ms suppression cooldown

The hover preview reappeared after stamp restore because a new stamp
created at the same position triggered enter-event immediately.
hidePreview() now suppresses new previews for 300ms, giving time for
the mouse to move away from the restored window's area.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Minimized windows become stamps on the shelf

Instead of vanishing, minimized windows are shrunk to 1x1 stamps and
placed on the shelf (or whatever placement mode is active). Clicking
the stamp unminimizes and restores the window to full size in the grid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix scaleFactor reference error and meta_window undefined crash

scaleManager.js: Removed stale reference to scaleFactor parameter

extension.js: Added defensive check for meta_window objects that

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stamp size, docking, drag snap, and preview dismissal

Stamps: Auto-shrunk stamps are now always 1x1 grid cell (true postage
stamp size). Manual zoom still produces scale-proportional clones.
createClone() takes target pixel dimensions instead of scale factor.

Preview: hidePreview() called in _restoreWindow before destroyClone
so the hover preview is always dismissed when a stamp is restored.

Drag snap: Use 50ms timeout instead of idle_add to ensure Mutter has
fully finalized the grab before overriding window position. Applies
to both tracked drag drops and resize snap fallback.

Shelf docking: placeWindowAt now respects reserved areas. Stamps pass
allowReserved=true. 1x1 stamps fit neatly into shelf edge cells.

Release v1.1.0

Add timeout to embedding model initialization (fixes #5)

When ctrl+c interrupts model loading, @xenova/transformers leaves stale
lock files in ~/.cache/huggingface/hub/.locks/ that cause subsequent
runs to hang indefinitely. Add a 30s timeout to the pipeline() call
and reset initPromise on failure so the service can retry.

The error message directs users to delete the stale lock files.

Migrate from CommonJS to ESM output (fixes #18)

Node.js 22+ triggers a CJS/ESM dual-package hazard when CommonJS code
requires the ESM-only @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, causing tools/list to
return empty. Fix by switching to ESM output throughout:

- package.json: add "type": "module"
- tsconfig.json: module/moduleResolution → NodeNext, target → ES2022
- All local imports: add .js extensions (required by NodeNext)
- jest.config.js → jest.config.cjs with moduleNameMapper to strip
  .js extensions for Jest's CJS resolver

Add PRIVATE_JOURNAL_PATH env var for configurable journal root

For containerized deployments where the default home directory
resolution doesn't apply. When set, both project and user journals
write to the specified path, bypassing all fallback logic.

Fix test expectations for embedding file generation

journal.test.ts: writeThoughts tests expected 1 file per directory but
embedding generation now creates .md + .embedding files. Updated counts
to 2 and switched from files[0] to .find(f => f.endsWith('.md')) for
reading content. Empty content test correctly expects 1 file since
empty entries skip embedding generation.

embeddings.test.ts: Semantic search ordering test was fragile — asserted
the frustration entry was the top result, but model ranking isn't
deterministic. Changed to assert it appears anywhere in results.

Add README, LICENSE, update .gitignore and metadata for GitHub release

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dismiss hover preview via pointer polling instead of leave-event

MetaWindowActor leave-event is unreliable. Now polls global.get_pointer()
every 100ms and dismisses when the cursor is outside both the stamp
container and the real window's frame rect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stamp clicks, auto-shrink when disabled, hover dismiss on mouse-out

Stamp clicks: Click handler now calls _restoreWindow() directly
instead of relying on focus events (which are suppressed during
hover). Also dismisses any active hover first.

Auto-shrink: _batchShrinkForSpace (called when grid is full) now
checks autoShrinkEnabled. When auto-shrink is off, windows won't
be force-shrunk to make room for new ones.

Hover dismiss: Added leave-event on the real window actor so the
hover preview hides when the mouse moves away from the full-size
window. Disconnect the handler on dismiss to avoid leaks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Keep stamp visible during hover, animate appearance, fix restore snap

Stamp visible: Container no longer goes opacity=0 during hover — the
stamp stays visible next to the full-size window so you can click it
to restore/un-minimize.

Animation: Hover window scales up from 30% to 100% over 200ms with
ease-out-quad for a less abrupt appearance.

Restore snap: Window position was unreliable after off-screen move
(get_monitor() returned wrong monitor). Now stores originalMonitor in
scale state and uses it during restore. destroyClone no longer tries
to restore position — _restoreWindow handles it via grid placement
and move_resize_frame.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show hover window next to stamp, not on top

Positioning the real window on top of the stamp blocked the stamp's
click-to-restore handler. Now tries: left of stamp, right, above,
below — with a 4px gap. The stamp stays visible and clickable for
restore/un-minimize while the real window is interactive next to it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move stamped windows off-screen to prevent transparent holes

MetaWindowActors at opacity=0 still participate in compositing,
creating transparent "holes" when other windows are dragged over them.

Fix: move the real window to (-width-100, -height-100) when creating
a stamp clone. Clutter.Clone renders from the source's paint output
regardless of position, so the stamp still shows live content. On
restore (destroyClone), the window moves back to its grid position.

During hover preview, the window is moved from off-screen to the
hover position. On dismiss, it goes back off-screen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hover shows real window, stays open until focus changes

Leave-event approach doesn't work — the real window appears above
the container in z-order and steals the mouse, firing leave-event
immediately.

New approach: hover opens the real window and it stays visible and
interactive until the user clicks on a different window. When focus
changes to a non-hovered window, the hover is dismissed (real window
re-hidden, stamp reappears). This is more natural UX — hover to peek,
interact, click elsewhere to dismiss.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expand stamp container to real window size during hover

The stamp container (1x1 cell) was too small — when the real window
appeared at full size elsewhere, the mouse was no longer over the
container, triggering immediate leave-event and re-hide.

Fix: expand the container to match the real window's size and
position during hover. The container stays reactive (opacity=0) so
leave-event fires when the mouse exits the full-size area, not the
stamp-size area. Clone inside is also resized to match. Both are
restored to original size on leave.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix hover to show real interactive window

Minimized windows need unminimize() to become visible — Mutter hides
minimized actors at the compositor level regardless of opacity. Hover
now unminimizes, activates, and positions the window. On leave, it
re-minimizes.

Focus and minimize handlers are suppressed during hover via
isHovering() check, preventing the unminimize/re-minimize from
triggering the full restore/re-grid flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stamp vanishing on hover — use opacity not visibility

Setting container.visible=false triggered leave-event immediately
(mouse "left" the invisible container), which re-hid the real window.
Now uses container.opacity=0 to make the clone transparent while
keeping it reactive for leave-event detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hover shows real interactive window, revert to clones for zoom

Reverted manual zoom to use clones (no content reflow). All shrunk
windows use the clone pattern for visual scaling.

Hover on a stamp now shows the REAL window at full size — un-hides
the actor (opacity=255, pick restored), positions it near the stamp,
and hides the clone container. The window is fully interactive: you
can click, type, scroll. On mouse leave, the real window is re-hidden
and the clone reappears.

This gives the best of both worlds: no reflow when shrunk, full
interactivity on hover.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Manual zoom resizes real window instead of creating clone

Manually zoomed windows (75%, 50%, 25%) are now resized via
move_resize_frame instead of creating a Clutter.Clone. This means:
- Windows stay interactive (clicks, typing work correctly)
- No visual glitching from clone rendering
- Drag and snap work at all scales
- Content reflows to fit the smaller size

Only auto-shrunk stamps and minimized windows use the clone pattern
(1x1 cell, non-interactive, click to restore).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

In-place stamp expansion on hover, zoom-out-to-minimize

Hover: Stamps expand to full window size in-place when the mouse
enters, and collapse back to stamp size when it leaves. No separate
preview clone — the stamp's own clone is resized. Much simpler and
more reliable than the old separate-preview approach.

Zoom: Pressing zoom-out (Super+-) at minimum scale (25%) now
minimizes the window, turning it into a stamp on the shelf. This
gives the full zoom progression: 100% → 75% → 50% → 25% → stamp.

Removed old hover-preview CSS class and separate-clone preview code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stamps vanishing on workspace switch

Removed switch-workspace handler that hid all stamps during
transition. Since stamps are in window_group, they slide with
the workspace naturally — no need to hide/show during animation.
Only active-workspace-changed is needed to toggle visibility
after the switch completes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Put stamps in window_group for proper workspace sliding

Stamps are back in window_group so they slide with workspace
transitions naturally. The meta_window crash is fixed by setting a
dummy meta_window property on the stamp layer that returns
NORMAL window type — GNOME Shell's layout.js only cares about
POPUP_MENU/DROPDOWN_MENU types, so NORMAL is safely ignored.

Also: hide all stamps during workspace switch animation
(switch-workspace signal), show correct workspace's stamps after
transition completes (active-workspace-changed signal). Set stamp
visibility on creation based on current workspace.

Preview popups use top_window_group (above everything).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add requirement-level extraction completeness guarantees

The previous coverage check only verified that spec files were cited by
stories — one broad story could make a whole chunk look covered while
dropping smaller requirements inside it.

Changes:
- Add PAR omission review pass after extraction, before aggregation.
  Two reviewers compare source text against extracted stories with the
  sole job of finding what's missing.
- Add coverage ledger: maps every spec chunk to specific story IDs with
  explicit classification (covered/non-normative/duplicate/gap).
  "Covered" means ACs correspond to normative content, not just that
  a story cites the file.
- Hard gate: bootstrap fails if any normative chunk is classified as gap.
- Add consolidation safety check: verify epic merge doesn't silently
  collapse distinct requirements via title-match deduplication.
- Reorder pipeline: omission review before aggregation so findings feed
  into the aggregate.

2. Add explicit story-splitting step after first PAR scope review —

feat: make Pepper Chat opt-in

Fix human_type: send keyDown/keyUp in headed mode only

In headless Chrome, rawKeyDown triggers browser-level keyboard shortcuts
(navigating to chrome://settings/help), breaking the page. In headed mode,
key events are correctly dispatched to the focused page element.

Now conditionally sends keyDown/keyUp events based on chromeHeadless:
- Headed: rawKeyDown + insertText + keyUp (full key event chain for bot detection)
- Headless: insertText only (per-character timing still provides bot-detection resistance)

Add human_type, double_click, right_click, and file_upload actions

- human_type: Character-by-character insertText with variable delays
  (~80-160ms per char). Handles uppercase and symbols. The per-character
  timing is the primary signal bot detectors check for vs bulk insertion.
- double_click: CDP mouse events with clickCount:2, fires dblclick event
- right_click: CDP mouse events with button:'right', fires contextmenu
- file_upload: DOM.setFileInputFiles for input[type=file] elements,
  the only way to programmatically set files (JS can't do it)

Add CDP-level mouse actions: hover, drag_drop, mouse_move, scroll

Adds four new browser actions that use Input.dispatchMouseEvent to
simulate hardware-level mouse input, bypassing the DataTransfer
restriction on synthetic JS DragEvents.

- hover: mouseMoved to element center (CSS :hover, tooltips, menus)
- drag_drop: mousePressed → mouseMoved steps → mouseReleased
  (native DnD pipeline, supports selector or coordinate targets)
- mouse_move: raw coordinate movement with optional interpolation
  (bot detection patterns, captcha puzzles)
- scroll: mouseWheel events with direction strings or delta values
  (natural scrolling vs JS scrollTo which bot detectors flag)

Fixes the reported issue where synthetic DragEvent objects have
neutered DataTransfer, causing drop handlers to receive empty data.

Ignore non-POLLIN poll events in input activity detection

Add EXTRA_CLAUDE_CONFIGS for aggregating synced remote machines

Users who already sync ~/.claude between machines (via rsync, Syncthing,
etc.) can now aggregate usage from all of them into a single report
without installing the client on every machine. Set EXTRA_CLAUDE_CONFIGS
to a comma-separated list of directories, each containing a projects/
subdirectory of Claude Code JSONL sessions, and the reporter will run
ccusage once per directory (via CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) and merge the results.

Running ccusage separately per config dir — rather than pointing a
single ccusage at all of them via a comma-joined CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR —
avoids OOM when the combined session history is large. Each subprocess
gets its own heap with --max-old-space-size=8192 for headroom.

Error handling degrades gracefully: if the local ccusage succeeds but a
remote dir fails (or vice versa), the successful data is still reported.
Only if every ccusage call fails does Claude collection count as failed.

Authored by Claude Code (Opus 4.6) at Jesse Vincent's direction.

Add OpenAI platform usage collection

Users who make OpenAI API calls outside of Codex CLI (custom scripts,
agents, third-party tools using their API keys) have no way to report
that usage today. This wires in platform.openai.com/usage via the
/v1/organization/usage/completions endpoint, gated on a new optional
OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY env var.

Requires an admin API key (sk-admin-...) because regular project keys
can't query org-level usage. Only completions usage is collected — that
covers chat completions and the Responses API, where ~all token volume
lives. Embeddings/moderations are separate endpoints and can be added
later if needed.

Tokens are split into inputTokens/outputTokens/cacheReadTokens to match
ccusage semantics (OpenAI's input_tokens field already includes cached,
so we subtract input_cached_tokens to get non-cached input). Source
tagged "openai-api" to distinguish from Codex and Claude data.

Documented the double-counting risk prominently: if Codex CLI is
authenticated with an API key rather than ChatGPT subscription auth, its
traffic already shows up in platform usage and enabling this alongside
Codex collection will double-count. README and .env.example both warn.

Tests cover the pure transformation (bucketsToDaily) with nine cases:
happy path, multi-model per day, cross-day sorting, zero-token skipping,
missing model fallback, empty inputs, missing results array, negative
clamp, and custom source tag. collectOpenAIUsage is exercised for the
no-key path; live API integration is left manual.

Release v1.11.0: visibility-aware selection, async eval, crash fix

Fix tabs.filter crash when Chrome returns non-array response

resolveWsUrl() and closeTab() called .filter()/.find() on the result of
chromeHttp('/json') without checking Array.isArray() first. When Chrome
returns a non-array (empty object, error), this crashes with
"tabs.filter is not a function".

getTabs() already had this guard; now resolveWsUrl() and closeTab() do too.

Fixes #27

Fix evaluate() to await async expressions

Added awaitPromise: true to the Runtime.evaluate CDP call in evaluate(),
matching what evaluateJson() already does. Without this, async expressions
(fetch, async IIFEs) returned the Promise object as undefined/[object Object]
instead of the resolved value.

Fix element selection to prefer visible elements over hidden ones

getElementSelector() now uses querySelectorAll/XPath snapshot iteration
to find all matches, then picks the first element with a non-zero
bounding rect. Falls back to first DOM match with a console.warn when
all matches have zero dimensions.

Fixes PRI-1127: CDP clicks on responsive pages were hitting hidden
mobile-layout elements at (0,0) instead of the visible desktop element.

feat: add engineering principles to implementer and code-quality reviewer

Address four problems observed in comparative review of iterative-development
output: protocol explosion, platform-fighting, flat file organization, and
god-object coordination creep.

Implementer gets proactive principles (earn every abstraction, work with the
platform, navigability, decompose coordination) plus self-review items.

Code-quality reviewer gets a new Engineering Health section with four SERIOUS
finding categories that counterbalance the existing boxing-in check's bias
toward more abstraction.

Cross-iteration dependency guidance tempered: prefer thinnest boundary, not
decomposed hierarchy.

feat(agent): emit tool_state snapshot on TOOL_CALL_END

Adds a structured side channel for tool executors that want to expose
post-mutation state to dashboards and other event-stream consumers
without forcing them to reconstruct state by replaying prior events.

Tools return ToolStateResult{Output, State} from their Exec; the
registry marshals State into ToolExecResult.ToolState, and the session
emitter copies it onto the TOOL_CALL_END event as `tool_state`. The LLM
only ever sees Output — tool_state is not threaded into the model's
context.

task_list append and update now emit the full store.View() snapshot so
consumers can render a checklist with ids, descriptions, statuses, and
dependencies on every event, regardless of whether they started
listening mid-session.

fix(agent): enforce RootDir boundary on write_file, edit_file, shell cwd

Previously resolve() returned absolute paths unchanged, so the write_file
and edit_file tools could write anywhere the OS allowed regardless of the
agent's working directory. ExecCommand accepted absolute workingDir paths
outside RootDir with no check. apply_patch was the only tool enforcing a
boundary.

The gap let a debug_fix agent edit the orchestrator toil's source tree
(definitions/workflows/verify.yaml, internal/engine/engine.go) from
inside a project worktree in run flint-oak-willow — edits that couldn't
take effect without a restart, causing the debugger loop to exhaust
max_loop_iterations.

Add resolveWrite() that cleans + absolutises + rejects paths outside
RootDir, wire it into WriteFile and EditFile. Reject non-descendant
workingDir in ExecCommand. Resolve symlinks on both sides of the
comparison via resolveSymlinksBestEffort so macOS /var -> /private/var
canonicalisation doesn't false-positive.

This is a partial fix. Shell tools can still escape via `cd /other &&
write-stuff` — that needs OS-level sandbox enforcement (bwrap /
sandbox-exec) and is explicitly not addressed here.

feat(catalog): cache-tier pricing + GetPrice API + responses-mode models

Surfaces pricing fields from the embedded LiteLLM catalog that ModelInfo
wasn't exposing:

- CacheReadInputCostPerMillion (all providers that support prompt caching)
- CacheCreation5mCostPerMillion (Anthropic)
- CacheCreation1hCostPerMillion (Anthropic extended-cache-ttl)

Adds a GetPrice(modelID) method on ModelCatalog (plus a DefaultPrice
convenience that hits the embedded catalog). Lookup order is exact match
first, then longest-prefix fallback so dated snapshots like
"claude-opus-4-5-20260101" resolve to the "claude-opus-4-5" family when
only the family entry has pricing. Returns (Price, false) for unknown
or unpriced models so callers can distinguish "free" from "unknown."

Catalog mode filter widened from {"chat"} to {"chat", "responses"} so
OpenAI's Responses-API-only models (gpt-5-codex, o3-deep-research,
gpt-5-pro) are no longer silently excluded. Those models couldn't be
priced before this change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(llm): normalize Usage semantics across providers

Three behavioral fixes to the provider adapters so llm.Usage has the
same meaning regardless of which provider handled a call:

1. InputTokens always means "new uncached input." OpenAI and
   openaicompat used to pass through the provider's cached-inclusive
   input count, so consumers billed cache reads at full input rate.
   Both adapters now subtract cached_tokens before storing.

2. openaicompat now extracts prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens into
   CacheReadTokens. Kimi, GLM, OpenRouter, and every other openaicompat
   wrapper previously dropped that field silently.

3. Char-count "estimate" for reasoning tokens moved from ReasoningTokens
   to a new ReasoningTokensEstimated field on Usage. The original
   estimate was additive to output_tokens (which already includes
   thinking on Anthropic), which double-counted against any consumer
   doing output_tokens + reasoning_tokens arithmetic. Native provider
   counts still flow into ReasoningTokens; the estimate is display-only.

Also: Anthropic adapter now parses cache_creation.ephemeral_5m_input_tokens
and ephemeral_1h_input_tokens when present, routing the 1h writes to a
new CacheWrite1hTokens field so pricing can apply the correct rate.
Falls back to the aggregate cache_creation_input_tokens (5m TTL) when
the breakdown isn't supplied.

TotalTokens invariant documented: InputTokens + OutputTokens +
CacheReadTokens + CacheWriteTokens + CacheWrite1hTokens. Anthropic's
Add method updated to include the new optional field.

feat(cli): add --output-schema flag for communicate.output

Replaces the SERF_SUBMIT_RESULT_REQUIRED_DATA_KEYS / SERF_COMMUNICATE_REQUIRED_DATA_KEYS
env var + key-name heuristic path with a new --output-schema CLI flag that
accepts a raw JSON Schema. The schema replaces the communicate tool's `output`
field schema wholesale — no inference, no merging — ending the silent data-loss
bug tracked in serf issue #2.

- Adds --output-schema to `serf` and `serf serve`. Parsed at flag time; parse
  failures report `serf: invalid --output-schema: <err>` and exit 1.
- Adds agent.WithCommunicateOutputSchema(p, schema) wrapper that deep-copies
  the communicate tool's Parameters, replaces properties.output with a deep
  copy of the caller's schema, and ensures "output" is in the top-level
  required list. Nil/empty schema is a no-op. Works for both baseProfile and
  anthropicProfile. Double-application silently replaces (documented in the
  function comment).
- Threads outputSchemaJSON through cmdutil.SelectProfile, whose signature
  changes to (provider, model, outputSchemaJSON string). Callers in
  cmd/serf, cmd/serf/serve.go, cmd/serf-tui/embedded.go, and cmd/serfeval
  all updated.
- Stacking: WithCommunicateOutputSchema runs first, WithAllowedDecisions
  runs after. SERF_ALLOWED_DECISIONS + --allowed-decisions remains honored
  independently. If both are applied, `decision` is injected into the
  user-supplied output.properties.
- Tests: adds TestWithCommunicateOutputSchema_{ReplacesOutput,
  MakesOutputRequired, NilOrEmpty_NoOp, Anthropic},
  TestWithAllowedDecisions_WithOutputSchema_BothApplied,
  TestSelectProfile_{NoSchema, ValidSchema, InvalidJSON, WhitespaceOnly},
  and TestRunInvalidOutputSchema as a black-box wire-through.

Reframe issue triage: bug reports are not product defects

Phase 6 previously described validation as "check whether each has
been resolved in the current codebase" via grep/git log searches.
This produces false-positive "still valid" verdicts because the test
was wrong: it verified the reporter's narration, not the actual bug.

Bug reports are reports of possible problems by unreliable narrators.
Reporters misdiagnose causes, point at the wrong files, attribute
symptoms to the wrong code, and describe environment-specific
behavior as universal. The fact that a reporter said "the bug is in
file X line Y" and file X line Y still says what they quoted does
NOT prove the bug exists. The bug is the behavior, not the narration.

Changes:
- Add foundational framing at the top: bug reports are hypotheses to
  be tested by reproduction, not facts to be verified by inspection
- Rewrite Phase 6 around reproduction by claim type (deterministic
  vs LLM-steering vs multi-step session vs harness-specific vs
  feature-request)
- New verdict taxonomy: REPRODUCED / NOT REPRODUCIBLE / NEED-REPRO-ENV
  / NEED-MORE-INFO / NOT-A-DEFECT
- New section "Tempting shortcuts that produce wrong verdicts" with
  the specific failure modes Claude has hit
- New section on dev-vs-main branch handling - closing on a dev
  commit message is a common false-close vector
- Five new entries in Common Mistakes covering the unreliable-narrator
  failure modes
